gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/188)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/188)
text: كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) يُعْجِبُهُ الْعِنَبُ فَكَانَ يَوْماً صَائِماً فَلَمَّا أَفْطَرَ كَانَ أَوَّلُ مَا جَاءَ الْعِنَبُ أَتَتْهُ أُمُّ وَلَدٍ لَهُ بِعُنْقُودِ عِنَبٍ فَوَضَعَتْهُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَجَاءَ سَائِلٌ فَدَفَعَهُ إِلَيْهِ فَدَسَّتْ أُمُّ وَلَدِهِ إِلَى السَّائِلِ فَاشْتَرَتْهُ مِنْهُ ثُمَّ أَتَتْهُ بِهِ فَوَضَعَتْهُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَجَاءَ سَائِلٌ آخَرُ فَأَعْطَاهُ إِيَّاهُ فَفَعَلَتْ أُمُّ الْوَلَدِ كَذَلِكَ ثُمَّ أَتَتْهُ بِهِ فَوَضَعَتْهُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَجَاءَ سَائِلٌ آخَرُ فَأَعْطَاهُ فَفَعَلَتْ أُمُّ الْوَلَدِ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ فَلَمَّا كَانَ فِي الْمَرَّةِ الرَّابِعَةِ أَكَلَهُ ( عليه السلام ).

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hisham Bin Salim who said,

‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> liked the grapes. So one day he<sup>as</sup> was Fasting, and when he<sup>as</sup> broke Fast the first of what came were the grapes, given to him<sup>asws</sup> by a mother of his<sup>as</sup> child, a cluster of grapes. So she placed these in front of him<sup>asws</sup>, and a beggar came over. So he<sup>asws</sup> handed these over to him, and the mother of his<sup>as</sup> child went to the beggar and bought them off from him. Then she came with it and placed it in front of him<sup>asws</sup>. So another beggar came over, and he<sup>asws</sup> gave these to him. So the mother of his<sup>as</sup> child did the like of that. Then she came with these and placed them in front of him<sup>as</sup>. So there came another beggar, and he<sup>asws</sup> gave these to him. So the mother of his<sup>asws</sup> child did the like of that. So when it was during the fourth time, he<sup>asws</sup> ate it (as no other beggar came over)’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Hisham ibn Salim who has said the following: “Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, liked grapes. One day he fasted and when it was the time to break his fast, the first thing that was brought to him was grapes. A mother of his child brought a bunch of grapes and placed before him. A beggar came and he (the Imam) give it to him. The mother of his child managed to buy it secretly from the beggar. She then placed it before him (the Imam). Another beggar came and he (the Imam) give it to the beggar. The mother of his child again secretly bought it from the beggar. She placed it before him (the Imam). Another beggar came and he (the Imam) give it to the beggar and she again secretly bought and placed it before him (the Imam). In the fourth time he (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was able to eat it.’”
